subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Differential Equations
stimulus: At time $t = 0$, a boiled potato is taken from a pot on a stove and left to cool in a kitchen. The internal temperature of the potato is $91$ degrees Celsius ($^\circ$C) at time $t = 0$, and the internal temperature of the potato is greater than $27^\circ$C for all times $t > 0$. The internal temperature of the potato at time $t$ minutes can be modeled by the function $H$ that satisfies the differential equation $$\frac{dH}{dt} = -\frac{1}{4}(H - 27),$$ where $H(t)$ is measured in degrees Celsius and $H(0) = 91$.

parts.prompt: Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $H$ at $t = 0$. Use this equation to approximate the internal temperature of the potato at time $t = 3$.

scoring_points.description: Computes $\frac{dH}{dt}\big|_{t=0}$ correctly using the differential equation with $H(0) = 91$
Uses the tangent line to approximate $H(3)$

parts.prompt: Use $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2}$ to determine whether your answer in part (a) is an underestimate or an overestimate of the internal temperature of the potato at time $t = 3$.

scoring_points.description: States underestimate with correct reasoning using the second derivative. Since $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2} = -\frac{1}{4}\frac{dH}{dt} = \frac{1}{16}(H - 27) > 0$, the graph of $H$ is concave up, so the tangent line lies below the curve and gives an underestimate.

required_elements: Graph of $H$ is concave up, so tangent line gives underestimate

scoring_points.correct_example: $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2} = -\frac{1}{4}\cdot(-\frac{1}{4}(H-27)) = \frac{1}{16}(H-27)$. Since $H > 27$ for all $t > 0$, $\frac{d^2H}{dt^2} > 0$, so $H$ is concave up and the tangent line approximation is an underestimate.

parts.prompt: For $t < 10$, an alternate model for the internal temperature of the potato at time $t$ minutes is the function $G$ that satisfies the differential equation $$\frac{dG}{dt} = -(G - 27)^{2/3},$$ where $G(t)$ is measured in degrees Celsius and $G(0) = 91$. Find an expression for $G(t)$. Based on this model, what is the internal temperature of the potato at time $t = 3$?
parts.scoring_notes: Note: max 2/5 [1-1-0-0-0] if no constant of integration. Note: 0/5 if no separation of variables.
